sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.title: Definitions of key terms for the Council
sections.summary: This section defines critical terms used throughout the Act, including 'Chairperson', 'Council', 'Executive Director', 'Intellectual property', 'member', 'Minister', 'Ministry', and 'research and development'. These definitions establish the scope and meaning of key entities and concepts for the Mauritius Emerging Technologies Council.

requirements.text: Chairperson means the Chairperson of the Council
Council means the Mauritius Emerging Technologies Council established under section 3
Executive Director means the Executive Director of the Council, appointed under section 10

Intellectual property includes any knowledge, know-how, technique, technology, design or trade secret, invented, developed or acquired as a result of research and development and innovation activities
Member means a member of the Council
Minister means the Minister to whom responsibility for the subject of information technology is assigned
Ministry means the Ministry responsible for the subject of information technology

Research and development means systematic, investigative and experimental activities which involve emerging technologies and are carried out, wholly or partly, within Mauritius for the purpose of creating new or improved materials, products, devices, processes or services

sections.source_quote: Interpretation In this Act – "Chairperson" means the Chairperson of the Council; "Council" means the Mauritius Emerging Technologies Council established under section 3; "Executive Director" means the Executive Director of the Council, appointed under section 10; "Intellectual property" includes any knowledge, know-how, technique, technology, design or trade secret, invented, developed or acquired as a result of research and development and innovation activities undertaken; "member" – (a) means a member of the Council; and (b) includes the Chairperson; "Minister" means the Minister to whom responsibility for the subject of information technology is assigned; "Ministry" means the Ministry responsible for the subject of information technology; "research and development" means systematic, investigative and experimental activities which involve emerging technologies and are carried out, wholly or partly, within Mauritius for the purpose of creating new or improved materials, products, devices, processes or services.

sections.heading: The Council
sections.title: Composition and term of Council members
sections.summary: This section specifies the composition of the Council, requiring a Chairperson appointed by the Minister, representatives from the Ministry, the Ministry of Finance, the Ministry of Industrial Development, the Attorney-General's Office, the Economic Development Board, the private sector, and four technology experts appointed by the Minister. It also outlines disqualifications for members, term lengths, resignation procedures, and conditions for ceasing office.

requirements.text: The Council shall consist of a Chairperson, to be appointed by the Minister
The Council shall consist of a representative of the Ministry
The Council shall consist of a representative of the Ministry responsible for the subject of finance
The Council shall consist of a representative of the Ministry responsible for the subject of industrial development
The Council shall consist of a representative of the Attorney-General's Office
The Council shall consist of a representative from the Economic Development Board
The Council shall consist of a representative from the private sector
The Council shall consist of 4 technology experts, to be appointed by the Minister
No person shall be qualified to be a member where he is a member of the Assembly
No person shall be qualified to be a member where he is a member of a local authority
No person shall be qualified to be a member where he is otherwise actively engaged in politics
Subject to subsection (4), a member, other than an ex officio member, shall hold office for a period of 3 years and may be eligible for reappointment
A member, other than an ex officio member, shall cease to hold office on the completion of his term of office
A member, other than an ex officio member, shall cease to hold office on his resignation
A member, other than an ex officio member, shall cease to hold office where he becomes a member of the Assembly or a local authority, or otherwise actively engages in politics
A member, other than an ex officio member, shall cease to hold office where, without sufficient cause, he fails to attend 3 consecutive meetings of the Council
Every member shall be paid such fees or allowances as the Minister may determine
The names of the members of the Council shall be published in the Gazette
The Council may co-opt any person who may be of assistance in relation to any matter before it and the co-opted member shall not have the right to vote at any meeting of the Council
The Council may co-opt any person who may be of assistance in relation to any matter before it and the co-opted member shall be paid such fees or allowances as the Council may determine
A member shall not engage in any activity which may undermine the reputation or the integrity of the Council

sections.source_quote: The Council shall consist of – (a) a Chairperson, to be appointed by the Minister; (b) a representative of the Ministry; (c) a representative of the Ministry responsible for the subject of finance; (d) a representative of the Ministry responsible for the subject of industrial development; (e) a representative of the Attorney-General's Office; (f) a representative of the Economic Development Board; (g) a representative from the private sector; and (h) 4 technology experts, to be appointed by the Minister. No person shall be qualified to be a member where he is – (a) a member of the Assembly; (b) a member of a local authority; or (c) otherwise actively engaged in politics. Subject to subsection (4), a member, other than an ex officio member, shall hold office for a period of 3 years and may be eligible for reappointment. A member, other than an ex officio member, shall cease to hold office – (a) on the completion of his term of office; (b) on his resignation; (c) where he becomes a member of the Assembly or a local authority, or otherwise actively engages in politics; or (d) where, without sufficient cause, he fails to attend 3 consecutive meetings of the Council. Every member shall be paid such fees or allowances as the Minister may determine. The names of the members of the Council shall be published in the Gazette. The Council may co-opt any person who may be of assistance in relation to any matter before it and the co-opted member shall – (a) not have the right to vote at any meeting of the Council; and (b) be paid such fees or allowances as the Council may determine. A member shall not engage in any activity which may undermine the reputation or the integrity of the Council.
sections.relevance_reason: irrelevant: governance structure, no data compliance elements

sections.heading: Disclosure of interest
sections.title: Disclosure of conflicts of interest
sections.summary: This section requires members or their relatives to disclose any pecuniary or material interest in matters before the Council, and prohibits them from participating in deliberations on those matters. Disclosures must be recorded in meeting minutes to ensure transparency and accountability.

requirements.text: Where any member, or any person related to the member by blood or marriage, has any pecuniary or other material interest in, or in relation to, any matter before the Council or a committee, that member shall disclose the nature of the interest before or at the meeting convened to discuss the matter

Where any member, or any person related to the member by blood or marriage, has any pecuniary or other material interest in, or in relation to, any matter before the Council or a committee, that member shall not take part in any deliberations relating to that matter
A disclosure of interest made under subsection (1) shall be recorded in the minutes of proceedings of the meeting of the Council or committee, as the case may be

sections.source_quote: Where any member, or any person related to the member by blood or marriage, has any pecuniary or other material interest in, or in relation to, any matter before the Council or a committee, that member shall – (a) disclose the nature of the interest before or at the meeting convened to discuss the matter; and (b) not take part in any deliberations relating to that matter. A disclosure of interest made under subsection (1) shall be recorded in the minutes of proceedings of the meeting of the Council or committee, as the case may be.
sections.relevance_reason: No data privacy or cross-border transfer relevance

sections.heading: General Fund
sections.title: Establishment of General Fund
sections.summary: This section mandates the Council to establish a General Fund that receives funds from the Consolidated Fund, loans, and other lawful sources, and uses these funds to make all required payments. It ensures financial accountability by specifying the fund's purpose and sources.

requirements.text: The Council shall set up a General Fund into which shall be paid any amount allocated out of the Consolidated Fund
The Council shall set up a General Fund into which shall be paid any loan granted or other sum which may lawfully accrue to the Council
The Council shall set up a General Fund into which shall be paid any sum received by the Council from any other source
Out of which all payments required to be made by it shall be effected

sections.source_quote: The Council shall set up a General Fund – (a) into which shall be paid – (i) any amount allocated out of the Consolidated Fund; (ii) any loan granted or other sum which may lawfully accrue to the Council; and (iii) any sum received by the Council from any other source; and (b) out of which all payments required to be made by it shall be effected.
sections.relevance_reason: financial accountability, no data compliance relevance

sections.heading: Annual report
sections.title: Annual reporting obligations
sections.summary: This section requires the Council to publish an annual report within six months of the financial year-end, including audited accounts. It also specifies the first financial year's definition, excludes certain accounting provisions, designates the Director of Audit as auditor, and mandates reporting to the Minister and the Assembly.

requirements.text: The Council shall, not later than 6 months after the close of every financial year, cause to be published a report on the activities of the Council, including its audited accounts, in respect of that financial year

For the purposes of the Statutory Bodies (Accounts and Audit) Act, the period extending from the commencement of this Act to 30 June of the following year shall be deemed to be the first financial year of the Council
Section 7 (1) of the Statutory Bodies (Accounts and Audit) Act shall not apply to the first financial year of the Council
The auditor to be appointed under section 5 (1) of the Statutory Bodies (Accounts and Audit) Act shall be the Director of Audit
The Council shall forward a copy of the report referred to in subsection (1) to the Minister
The Council shall furnish to the Minister such information with respect to its activities, in such manner and at such time, as he may specify
The Minister shall, at the earliest available opportunity, lay a copy of the annual report and audited accounts of the Council before the Assembly

sections.source_quote: The Council shall, not later than 6 months after the close of every financial year, cause to be published a report on the activities of the Council, including its audited accounts, in respect of that financial year. For the purposes of the Statutory Bodies (Accounts and Audit) Act, the period extending from the commencement of this Act to 30 June of the following year shall be deemed to be the first financial year of the Council. Section 7 (1) of the Statutory Bodies (Accounts and Audit) Act shall not apply to the first financial year of the Council. The auditor to be appointed under section 5 (1) of the Statutory Bodies (Accounts and Audit) Act shall be the Director of Audit. The Council shall forward a copy of the report referred to in subsection (1) to the Minister. The Council shall furnish to the Minister such information with respect to its activities, in such manner and at such time, as he may specify. The Minister shall, at the earliest available opportunity, lay a copy of the annual report and audited accounts of the Council before the Assembly.
